core_mechanism: a diffusion / Fokker-Planck partial differential equation governing the evolution of a probability density, in which a resolution/scale parameter plays the role of the time variable, so that coarse-graining across scales spreads and reshapes the distribution
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as a multi-scale stochastic-process model that solves for the probability density of contact pressure and interfacial gap via partial differential equations, i.e. a scale-parametrized diffusion equation for an evolving probability distribution.
